stories.title: Jurgen Klopp warns German media ‘leave my family in peace’ or he will ‘leave’ national team
stories.summary: Jurgen Klopp has warned the media that if they “behave badly” he will leave the job on his unveiling as new Germany head coach. The former Liverpool coach addressed journalists in a press conference on Friday, marking his return to management more than two years after he left the Liverpool dugout. Klopp outlined his conditions for staying in the role to the media, saying if they “don’t want me anymore” or “don’t leave my family in peace” he will be “gone”. The direct address to the press comes after his predecessor Julian Nagelsmann faced intense scrutiny and criticism from media and former members of the national team, intruding into his private life while in the role.

stories.title: Jurgen Klopp: Germany appoint former Liverpool boss as new head coach to replace Julian Nagelsmann
stories.summary: Germany have appointed Jurgen Klopp as their new head coach. Klopp returns to management on a contract that runs until the end of the 2030 World Cup after leaving Liverpool in 2024 because he was "running out of energy" , having won six trophies in nine years at the club. After a six-month break, the 59-year-old was appointed Red Bull's head of global soccer, later stating he had no desire to return to club management. The 59-year-old was unveiled on Friday and issued a stark warning to potential critics that he would walk away from the job if he and his family are treated inappropriately. Transfer Centre LIVE! | Latest on YOUR PL club! Not got Sky?

stories.title: Chelsea news: Maxence Lacroix set to join Blues - have your say
stories.summary: Chelsea have targeted Maxence Lacroix after two impressive seasons in English football. During the 2025-26 Premier League campaign, he was the only player to record at least 50 tackles (60), 200 duels won (204) and 250 clearances (252). At 6ft 4in, Lacroix combines aerial strength with exceptional recovery pace. Since arriving from Wolfsburg in 2024, he has ranked among the five fastest centre-backs in the Premier League, recording a top speed of 35. 81km/h (22. 3mph). That athleticism is reflected in his defensive numbers.

stories.title: Tour de France Braces for Chaos as Fans Flood Alpe d’Huez for Historic Double
stories.summary: Updated July 24, 2026 04:36AM The Tour de France peloton is bracing madness and pandemonium on Alpe d’Huez . Fans started arriving days ago, and an estimated 500,000 have already poured into the ski resort for back-to-back stage finishes on the Tour’s most famous climb. Overnight videos on social media showed fans partying deep into the night, and Visma-Lease a Bike boss Richard Plugge raised the alarm about something going wrong. “There are now also a lot of people who think it’s carnival, who fill up with beer all day and at some point don’t know what they’re doing,” Plugge told In de Leiderstrui . “That’s really going to be a problem.

stories.title: France sends boats to evacuate tourist hotspot Cap Ferret as wildfires rage
stories.summary: (CNN) — French authorities ordered the complete evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula on Friday, launching boats to help rescue people from the popular tourist destination that’s now threatened by wildfires. Evacuation boats operated from three piers on the peninsula, which is situated about 70 kilometers (43 miles) west of Bordeaux in southwestern France, the province’s prefect, Sophie Brocas, said Friday morning. Such an operation demonstrates the severity of the wildfires currently ripping across parts of southern France and elsewhere in Europe, fueled by record-breaking , climate change-driven heat waves that have turned the landscape into a tinderbox.

stories.title: Intriguing details but few answers in Government’s Aughinish Alumina report
stories.summary: On Friday morning, the Government released its long-awaited investigation into whether raw material from the Aughinish Alumina plant in Co Limerick is ending up in Russian weapons. Or rather, it released a 450-word summary of the report in the form of a statement by Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke. Releasing the full report would compromise commercially sensitive information provided by Aughinish, Burke said. Anyone hoping for definitive answers in the terse summary will be disappointed. After four months of investigating, department officials have found no evidence to prove alumina is being used by Moscow’s military machine. By the same token, they can’t rule it out.

stories.title: Love Island's Elicia hits back at Priya's 'classist' comments about National Rail: "Bitter and jealous"
stories.summary: Love Island's Elicia has hit out at Priya over comments she made during the Grafties about her job. ICYMI, 25-year-old Priya, a business development manager from Surrey, has been at the centre of a classism row after making a dig at dumped bombshell Elicia, a 23-year-old from Rotherham's work as a team organiser at National Rail. During a recent challenge called the Grafties, where the remaining Islanders were nominated for different categories like 'biggest flirt' and 'most emotional moment', a clip of Elicia on Love Island Aftersun was played. In the clip, Maya Jama asked her who she thought the biggest game player in the villa was, and she said it was Priya.

stories.title: Pep Guardiola responds to Italy job offer after meeting with Paolo Maldini
stories.summary: News Images 24 Jul 2026 06:40-04:00 Pep Guardiola has declined an offer to become the head coach of the Italy national team following discussions with technical director Paolo Maldini. The former Manchester City manager opted to stick to his plan of taking an extended break from management rather than taking charge of the Azzurri ahead of their upcoming campaigns. Guardiola rejects Italy approach Guardiola has officially turned down an offer from the Italian Football Federation (FIGC) to coach the national team following direct talks with newly appointed technical director Maldini.

stories.title: Darren Purse takes over at Sligo Rovers
stories.summary: Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 09:22 Sligo Rovers have appointed Darren Purse as manager Former Premier League defender Darren Purse has been appointed as the new Sligo Rovers manager on an 18-month contract. Purse, who played for two seasons with Birmingham City in the English top flight, arrives at the Showgrounds from Cardiff City, where he spent almost five years working with the club's Under-23 and U21 sides, running their loan programme and serving a spell as assistant manager of the first team. He replaces John Russell, who ended an eight-year spell with the Connacht club to take over at Shelbourne earlier this month.

stories.title: Alonso shares expectations for Aston Martin updates with ‘zero doubt’ Newey will deliver
stories.summary: Fernando Alonso has made it clear that “no package in the world” will make up for the deficit Aston Martin faced to the cars ahead last time out in Belgium, but hopes their long-awaited upgrades can put the team on a “good path”. Aston Martin ’s challenging 2026 season reached a new low at the sweeping Spa-Francorchamps circuit when Alonso and team mate Lance Stroll were cut adrift from the midfield pack by some two seconds, and were more than four seconds down on the Q1 benchmark.

stories.title: House of the Dragon's James Norton responds to question over AI use in the show
stories.summary: House of the Dragon star James Norton has addressed claims of AI use in the hit fantasy show. Criticism was levied towards the Game of Thrones spin-off when it launched an AI poster generator that allowed viewers to pick a side in the Targaryen war. Fans have also regularly questioned whether AI is used to bring some of the more fantastical elements of the show to life, particularly the dragons that feature prominently. Speaking about the show, Ormund Hightower actor Norton addressed these claims when explaining how elements of House of the Dragon come together.

stories.title: The Strokes: Reality Awaits review – a perfect marriage of mayhem and melody on their best album in years
stories.summary: H ere’s a sentence no old-head Strokes fan wants to hear: to understand Reality Awaits, the stalwart NYC group’s seventh album and first in six years, you first have to understand the Voidz. Julian Casablancas’s other band has been taking up more and more of his time in recent years, much to the chagrin of fans who may have dipped into the group’s painfully, awkwardly proggy 2014 debut Tyranny and decided to just let the old legend have his fun (away from their own speakers, of course).

stories.title: Antonelli reveals issue he must fix after father’s warning
stories.summary: Relationships between Formula 1 drivers and their fathers can be complicated. Sometimes the latter prefer to adopt a “tough love” approach that can raise a few eyebrows. Kimi Antonelli and his father Marco could be the poster boys for the opposite approach, such is the apparent closeness of their relationship from the outside. And yet, as the Mercedes driver revealed on media day in Hungary , his father is also his biggest critic – and isn’t afraid to say what he thinks. In this case the context was Antonelli’s predilection for making full use of track limits, something he has on occasion been penalised for.

stories.title: Revealed: Lorenzo's wild secret life before Love Island, with connections to Zayn Malik and Ekin-Su
stories.summary: It would seem that before Love Island 2026, Lorenzo Alessi had a wild life he’s been keeping quiet. When he was first introduced as part of the cast this year , Lorenzo was simply described as 28, and a business owner from Hertfordshire. However, he actually has multiple celeb connections, and a very vibey life that he’s swept under the rug. We all know that Lorenzo is a charmer, we’ve seen it in 4k every night, but it turns out he’s a very well connected man outside the villa. He has ties to lots of people, in high-up places. And they do say it’s all about who you know!

stories.title: Mikel Arteta axes three Arsenal stars ahead of new season as squad revamp gathers pace
stories.summary: Three Arsenal players have already been axed by Mikel Arteta as the Spaniard continues his major squad overhaul this summer . The Gunners boss went into this transfer window knowing he had to improve the quality of his squad in order to retain the Premier League title next season. The first step in achieving this is to offload players who are considered to fall short of the required standard, while the second is to bring in signings that address gaps in the squad. Jakub Kiwior was the first player shown the door by Arteta this summer. The Pole completed a permanent switch to Porto earlier this month for £14. 6million plus add-ons , following a loan spell with the Portuguese club last season.

stories.title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Review: Exactly What ‘Big Bang Theory’ Fans Deserve
stories.summary: If Sheldon, Leonard, Raj and Wolowitz were congregating around the couch eating takeout, “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” is exactly the kind of series they’d have on the TV. HBO Max’s 10-part “Big Bang Theory” spinoff revolves around comic book store owner Stuart David Bloom (Kevin Sussman) and launches in utter destruction, with zombies and giant moths that only bother you if you leave the lights on.
stories.trend: stuart fails to save the universe

stories.title: MetroStar's IRIS modernises US Air Force KC-135 tanker servicing
stories.summary: The US Air Force has purchased MetroStar and ActionStreamer’s new Integrated Respirator Information System (IRIS) to help its maintainers better service its fleet of KC-135 tankers . This is one of the first modernisations of a half-century-old maintenance process. USAF purchases Integrated Respirator Information System for KC-135 Last Monday, the US contractor MetroStar announced that Travis Air Force Base (home to many tanker aircraft) had purchased its Iris, an AI-powered maintenance product co-produced with ActionStreamer. These are purpose-built equipment for high-risk confined-space work.

stories.title: Why Cheddar’s partner Azad Cola moved to take over No.1 Oxford Street Hotel
stories.summary: Cola Holdings Limited secured a High Court order granting its Receiver police assistance to take control of the Osu hotel linked to Nana Kwame Bediako The dispute centres on an alleged debt Cola Holdings claims it settled on behalf of Kensington Residential Partners 1 Limited, the company behind the hotel Cheddar has disputed personal liability for the debt while KRP1 has filed an appeal and applied for interim relief pending the court's determination The No. 1 Oxford Street Hotel in Osu, Accra, has become the subject of a legal battle after Cola Holdings Limited moved to seize control of the luxury property associated with entrepreneur Nana Kwame Bediako , widely known as Cheddar.

stories.title: Malaysia moves closer to VL MICA missiles procurement for LCS
stories.summary: The Malaysian Ministry of Defence (MINDEF) has officially issued a Letter of Acceptance ( Surat Setuju Terima or SST) to MBDA France for the procurement of the VL MICA surface-to-air missile (SAM) system. The agreement was finalized during the opening day of the Farnborough International Airshow 2026 in the United Kingdom. A formal contract is still pending. The document was signed by MINDEF Secretary-General Datuk Lokman Hakim Ali on behalf of the Malaysian government.

stories.title: Julia Barretto Moves Effortlessly in Onitsuka Tiger Spring/Summer 2026 Collection
stories.summary: NOTE: BrandNews articles are promotional features from our sponsors and not news articles from our editorial staff. ABS-CBN Newsletter Your go-to source for the latest news, entertainment highlights, exclusive interviews, and updates on your favorite Kapamilya stars.

stories.title: What's that planned next to Pet Styles on Kirby Whitten in Bartlett?
stories.summary: Baptist Urgent Care plans to open a new walk-in clinic adjacent to Pet Styles Grooming Salon on Kirby Whitten road in Bartlett, opening mid-August and offering services for minor illnesses and injuries without appointments.

stories.title: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion?
stories.summary: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion? An open letter to the pastors of Santa Clarita: “If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacking in daily food, and one of you says, ‘Go in peace, be warmed and filled,’ without giving them the things needed for the body, what good is that? ” — James 2:15-16. Here’s a question I can’t stop asking. What are your churches doing this week for the people in Santa Clarita who are too afraid to leave their homes? I’m not talking about politics. I’m not talking about immigration policy.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street. Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee first launched in 2008 to respond to economic challenges around the UK. The meeting will bring together senior ministers and regional mayors to "put devolution at the centre of the government's mission to make Britain better". Ahead of that meeting, the prime minister said: "For 40 years, power and resources have been sucked into the centre, and too many communities have felt forgotten, without the attention and investment they deserve. " He added: "The days of Whitehall resisting devolution are over, for good.

stories.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
stories.summary: Ramaphosa has faced scrutiny following the theft of large sums of cash from his private farm in 2020. A South African court has ruled in favour of President Cyril Ramaphosa's bid to temporarily halt a parliamentary inquiry looking into whether he should be impeached over what has been dubbed the "Farmgate" scandal. In 2022, an independent panel said Ramaphosa might have a case to answer after burglars stole huge sums of cash, hidden in a sofa, from his private farm. The president denied any wrongdoing. At the time MPs voted against establishing an impeachment inquiry, but in May the Constitutional Court ruled that parliament had acted unlawfully by shelving the panel's report.

stories.title: Managing Liverpool as Rayan signing secured and four stars are ousted by Andoni Iraola
stories.summary: Andoni Iraola is gearing up for his first campaign as Liverpool manager. A blockbuster £100m-plus winger signing could be just what he needs to get his tenure rolling. On the back of a fairly disappointing season, Arne Slot is out and Iraola is in at Anfield. His squad have jetted off for their pre-season tour of the United States. When they return, regaining the Premier League crown will surely be the goal, with Champions League football to contend with as well. Yet, Mohamed Salah's, Andy Robertson's and Ibrahima Konate's exits have left voids that need filling. Jeremy Jacquet has been given the No.

stories.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot as wildfires spread
stories.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula. Further down the Atlantic coast, around the town of Biscarrosse in France's Landes region, another major fire that began on Thursday afternoon has prompted the evacuations of more than 23,000 people. About 18,000 people in Biscarrosse and 5,000 in Parentis-en-Born had to leave their homes, campsites, a nursing home and a summer camp. Landes prefect Gilles Clavreul said the fire around Biscarrosse was not going to be out by the end of Friday, with winds of up to 50km/h (31mph) and temperatures of 36-37C.

stories.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
stories.summary: UK petrol and diesel prices are going up again as the price of oil returns to around $100 a barrel. According to the RAC, the average price of petrol reached an Iran war peak of 159. 53p a litre on 28 May, while diesel's highest average price during the conflict was 191. 54p a litre on 15 April. In early July, the RAC said the average price of diesel sank to a low of 150. 59p per litre for petrol and 164. 52p per litre for diesel. Since then the prices have been rising and according to its latest data, petrol now costs 156. 19p a litre while diesel costs 173. 15p a litre.

stories.title: Fred Tsui Named Executive Director of Hong Kong International Film Festival Society
stories.summary: The Hong Kong International Film Festival Society has appointed Fred Tsui , founder and CEO of Moebius Entertainment , as its new executive director, the organization said Friday. Tsui succeeds Albert Lee and assumes the role Sept. 23, reporting to HKIFFS chair Dr. Wilfred Wong and the board. In the new post, Tsui will oversee the society’s full range of initiatives: its marquee event, the Hong Kong International Film Festival, along with the Hong Kong – Asia Film Financing Forum and its HAF Film Lab, the Summer International Film Festival and the HKIFF Cine Fan strand.
entities: Hong Kong International Film Festival Society

stories.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
stories.summary: Jurgen Klopp starts his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. Jurgen Klopp started his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. The former Liverpool manager was appointed on a four-year deal on Friday to replace Julian Nagelsmann, who resigned this month after Germany's shock World Cup defeat by Paraguay in the last 32. But the 59-year-old issued a warning to the media as he held his first press conference as Germany's head coach. "If you behave badly and don't leave my family in peace then I'll be gone, I'll just turn away," Klopp said.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters hold talks with government as stalemate continues
stories.summary: The meeting came hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike. Thousands of people continue to protest demanding the Indian education minister's resignation after representatives of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) held talks with the government. CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka told reporters that the government has asked for a day to decide on their biggest demand - minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. "The protest will continue and will keep growing until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns. If that does not happen soon, we will be forced to announce another nationwide protest call," said Ranka.

stories.title: Meet the Glasgow curry shop boss representing Pakistan at bowls
stories.summary: Mohammed 'Chico' Qureshi has called Glasgow home for more than 50 years, but he will represent Pakistan against Scotland at the Commonwealth Games on Friday. It was something of a dream for Qureshi to go again in a second home Games after taking to the outdoor green at Kelvingrove 12 years ago. A member at Glasgow Indoor Bowling Club - just round the corner from his restaurant - he had been putting in the hours to be ready for the conditions awaiting him in his opening match against Wales' Ross Owen. Unfortunately, it did not quite go to plan as he lost in straight sets - 12-5 and 17-0. "That's the worst game I've ever played," a still upbeat Qureshi told BBC Scotland.

stories.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
stories.summary: The housing secretary tells the BBC that the Renters Rights' Act is already having a significant impact on the market. Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has said Prime Minister Andy Burnham is not looking at bringing in rent controls in England. She told BBC Breakfast that changes made through the Renters' Rights Act, which came into effect in May, were "already having a significant impact on the market". She added that controls which had been implemented in some areas of the UK over the past few years had not "necessarily brought rents down". In 2023 when he was the mayor of Greater Manchester, Burnham called for freezes to private sector rents until the cost of living crisis was over.

stories.title: Boxers choose Commonwealths over Love Island chance
stories.summary: Team Northern Ireland boxers Jon McConnell and Kaci Rock talk about choosing the Commonwealth Games over potentially appearing on Love Island. It could have been a very different summer for two members of Northern Ireland's 12-strong boxing team. Jon McConnell and Kaci Rock will compete in this year's Commonwealth Games for the first time, but had progressed through the initial auditions for the latest series of Love Island before pulling out because the TV show clashes in timing with the Games.

stories.title: Unlike Joe Duffy, Kieran Cuddihy doesn’t ramp up the drama on Liveline
stories.summary: For any broadcaster, the ability to see both sides of the story is a crucial skill, but it’s mandatory on a show such as Liveline ( RTÉ Radio 1 , weekdays), where everyone basically thinks they’re in the right. Even so, Kieran Cuddihy goes above and beyond in his duty as host of the phone-in show, going one step farther by contradicting his own argument even as he makes it. On Tuesday, as callers debate whether helmets and high-vis gear should be compulsory for cyclists , Cuddihy suggests that legislating on such matters is meaningless without proper enforcement. As an example, he points to the legal requirement for lights on bikes after dark. “It’s not enforced.

stories.title: Stuttgart divided over Ermedin Demirovic’s future amid interest from Leeds and others
stories.summary: The Sky Germany Transfer Update Show reports that Ermedin Demirovic’s future at VfB Stuttgart has become the subject of internal debate amid interest from Juventus, Fenerbahçe and Leeds United. Evidently, head coach Sebastian Hoeneß is open to selling the 28-year-old. Stuttgart’s administrative team nevertheless retain a different view. Stuttgart are presently planning with a three-striker rotation featuring Demirovic, Deniz Undav and Wolfsburg talent Dzenan Pejcinovic. The latter has already agreed personal terms, though the clubs have yet to finalize a transfer. Jeremy Arevalo would serve as a developmental option, while Jovan Milosevic remains free to leave.

stories.title: With Hopes High for New H.I.V. Prevention Pill, Merck Takes Steps to Ensure Access
stories.summary: Companies in Africa and India will make generic versions of the pill that could cost as little as $5 per person per year. But access to the drug and other new products in much of Latin America remains uncertain.

stories.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
stories.summary: ‘Bulking’, or gaining weight on purpose to get stronger, challenged everything I was once taught to do with my body I had been casually lifting weights for a few years when several things changed in quick succession. First, my boyfriend proposed. Continue reading. I had been casually lifting weights for a few years when several things changed in quick succession. This wasn’t a surprise – he had already told me he wanted to get married, and I had told him exactly how I wanted him to propose: somewhere public but not crowded, not in a restaurant.

stories.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
stories.summary: Microsoft has intervened to stop Windows 11 users with LG monitors from being bombarded with annoying McAfee trial pop-ups. In response to complaints about the LG bloatware, Microsoft’s Windows chief, Pavan Davuluri, said that LG has agreed to immediately disable the McAfee pop-up from its LG Monitor App Installer, and pledged that Microsoft will “keep improving here with our ecosystem partners. ” This crackdown follows backlash about large pop-up ads for McAfee antivirus and LG’s own apps appearing on Windows 11 PCs after being connected to LG monitors.

stories.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
stories.summary: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages. Iran says it has carried out drone strikes on US military facilities and bases across the Middle East in retaliation for the 13th consecutive night of American attacks on the country. Explosions were reported along Iran's Gulf coast, and in western, northern and central areas. Four people were killed and five injured in an attack in the south-west, Iranian state media said. Iran later announced strikes on US-allied Kuwait, while sirens sounded in Bahrain and Jordan, and flights were suspended at a major Iraqi airport after a reported drone crash.

stories.title: Why does Zapatero reappear now?
stories.summary: Why does Zapatero reappear now? This Thursday is an anniversary day. It has been exactly 26 years since José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero was elected secretary general of the PSOE. At that time, he did not imagine that a quarter of a century later he would be sitting in the studio of "Mañaneros" on TVE explaining himself for two serious criminal cases: one for alleged influence peddling for the Plus Ultra bailout and another for alleged smuggling of millionaire jewelry found in the safe of his office. Why has it appeared now? In fact, the explanations, according to what he himself had announced, should have come earlier.

stories.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners in the latest escalation in the trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year. Those targeted, including the UK, China and the European Union, will face a tariff of 10% to 12. 5% on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. The tariffs, which replace an identical levy which expires on Friday, are based on claims key economic partners have failed to properly tackle forced labour.

stories.title: An A.I. Music F.A.Q.: Can I Remix Madonna? Is This All Legal?
stories.summary: Advances in A.I. are making it possible to create all kinds of music from scratch, but they also raise questions about what is legal and who will actually listen.

stories.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
stories.summary: The divorce, involving the chairman of one of South Korea's biggest companies, has gripped the nation. The chairman of corporate giant SK Group has been ordered by a South Korean court to pay his ex-wife 944bn won (£483m; $644m) in a case that local media has called the " divorce of the century ". The award, which still needs to be finalised, is below the initial 1. 38tn won that chairman Chey Tae-won was told to pay Roh Soh-yeong in 2024. It comes more than a decade after his marriage to Roh - the daughter of a former president - fell apart following the revelation he had fathered a child with another woman.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

offbeat.title: Jurgen Klopp warns German media ‘leave my family in peace’ or he will ‘leave’ national team
offbeat.summary: Jurgen Klopp has warned the media that if they “behave badly” he will leave the job on his unveiling as new Germany head coach. The former Liverpool coach addressed journalists in a press conference on Friday, marking his return to management more than two years after he left the Liverpool dugout. Klopp outlined his conditions for staying in the role to the media, saying if they “don’t want me anymore” or “don’t leave my family in peace” he will be “gone”. The direct address to the press comes after his predecessor Julian Nagelsmann faced intense scrutiny and criticism from media and former members of the national team, intruding into his private life while in the role.

offbeat.title: Jurgen Klopp: Germany appoint former Liverpool boss as new head coach to replace Julian Nagelsmann
offbeat.summary: Germany have appointed Jurgen Klopp as their new head coach. Klopp returns to management on a contract that runs until the end of the 2030 World Cup after leaving Liverpool in 2024 because he was "running out of energy" , having won six trophies in nine years at the club. After a six-month break, the 59-year-old was appointed Red Bull's head of global soccer, later stating he had no desire to return to club management. The 59-year-old was unveiled on Friday and issued a stark warning to potential critics that he would walk away from the job if he and his family are treated inappropriately. Transfer Centre LIVE! | Latest on YOUR PL club! Not got Sky?

offbeat.title: Chelsea news: Maxence Lacroix set to join Blues - have your say
offbeat.summary: Chelsea have targeted Maxence Lacroix after two impressive seasons in English football. During the 2025-26 Premier League campaign, he was the only player to record at least 50 tackles (60), 200 duels won (204) and 250 clearances (252). At 6ft 4in, Lacroix combines aerial strength with exceptional recovery pace. Since arriving from Wolfsburg in 2024, he has ranked among the five fastest centre-backs in the Premier League, recording a top speed of 35. 81km/h (22. 3mph). That athleticism is reflected in his defensive numbers.

offbeat.title: Tour de France Braces for Chaos as Fans Flood Alpe d’Huez for Historic Double
offbeat.summary: Updated July 24, 2026 04:36AM The Tour de France peloton is bracing madness and pandemonium on Alpe d’Huez . Fans started arriving days ago, and an estimated 500,000 have already poured into the ski resort for back-to-back stage finishes on the Tour’s most famous climb. Overnight videos on social media showed fans partying deep into the night, and Visma-Lease a Bike boss Richard Plugge raised the alarm about something going wrong. “There are now also a lot of people who think it’s carnival, who fill up with beer all day and at some point don’t know what they’re doing,” Plugge told In de Leiderstrui . “That’s really going to be a problem.

offbeat.title: France sends boats to evacuate tourist hotspot Cap Ferret as wildfires rage
offbeat.summary: (CNN) — French authorities ordered the complete evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula on Friday, launching boats to help rescue people from the popular tourist destination that’s now threatened by wildfires. Evacuation boats operated from three piers on the peninsula, which is situated about 70 kilometers (43 miles) west of Bordeaux in southwestern France, the province’s prefect, Sophie Brocas, said Friday morning. Such an operation demonstrates the severity of the wildfires currently ripping across parts of southern France and elsewhere in Europe, fueled by record-breaking , climate change-driven heat waves that have turned the landscape into a tinderbox.

offbeat.title: Intriguing details but few answers in Government’s Aughinish Alumina report
offbeat.summary: On Friday morning, the Government released its long-awaited investigation into whether raw material from the Aughinish Alumina plant in Co Limerick is ending up in Russian weapons. Or rather, it released a 450-word summary of the report in the form of a statement by Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke. Releasing the full report would compromise commercially sensitive information provided by Aughinish, Burke said. Anyone hoping for definitive answers in the terse summary will be disappointed. After four months of investigating, department officials have found no evidence to prove alumina is being used by Moscow’s military machine. By the same token, they can’t rule it out.

offbeat.title: Love Island's Elicia hits back at Priya's 'classist' comments about National Rail: "Bitter and jealous"
offbeat.summary: Love Island's Elicia has hit out at Priya over comments she made during the Grafties about her job. ICYMI, 25-year-old Priya, a business development manager from Surrey, has been at the centre of a classism row after making a dig at dumped bombshell Elicia, a 23-year-old from Rotherham's work as a team organiser at National Rail. During a recent challenge called the Grafties, where the remaining Islanders were nominated for different categories like 'biggest flirt' and 'most emotional moment', a clip of Elicia on Love Island Aftersun was played. In the clip, Maya Jama asked her who she thought the biggest game player in the villa was, and she said it was Priya.

offbeat.title: Pep Guardiola responds to Italy job offer after meeting with Paolo Maldini
offbeat.summary: News Images 24 Jul 2026 06:40-04:00 Pep Guardiola has declined an offer to become the head coach of the Italy national team following discussions with technical director Paolo Maldini. The former Manchester City manager opted to stick to his plan of taking an extended break from management rather than taking charge of the Azzurri ahead of their upcoming campaigns. Guardiola rejects Italy approach Guardiola has officially turned down an offer from the Italian Football Federation (FIGC) to coach the national team following direct talks with newly appointed technical director Maldini.

offbeat.title: Darren Purse takes over at Sligo Rovers
offbeat.summary: Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 09:22 Sligo Rovers have appointed Darren Purse as manager Former Premier League defender Darren Purse has been appointed as the new Sligo Rovers manager on an 18-month contract. Purse, who played for two seasons with Birmingham City in the English top flight, arrives at the Showgrounds from Cardiff City, where he spent almost five years working with the club's Under-23 and U21 sides, running their loan programme and serving a spell as assistant manager of the first team. He replaces John Russell, who ended an eight-year spell with the Connacht club to take over at Shelbourne earlier this month.

offbeat.title: Alonso shares expectations for Aston Martin updates with ‘zero doubt’ Newey will deliver
offbeat.summary: Fernando Alonso has made it clear that “no package in the world” will make up for the deficit Aston Martin faced to the cars ahead last time out in Belgium, but hopes their long-awaited upgrades can put the team on a “good path”. Aston Martin ’s challenging 2026 season reached a new low at the sweeping Spa-Francorchamps circuit when Alonso and team mate Lance Stroll were cut adrift from the midfield pack by some two seconds, and were more than four seconds down on the Q1 benchmark.

offbeat.title: House of the Dragon's James Norton responds to question over AI use in the show
offbeat.summary: House of the Dragon star James Norton has addressed claims of AI use in the hit fantasy show. Criticism was levied towards the Game of Thrones spin-off when it launched an AI poster generator that allowed viewers to pick a side in the Targaryen war. Fans have also regularly questioned whether AI is used to bring some of the more fantastical elements of the show to life, particularly the dragons that feature prominently. Speaking about the show, Ormund Hightower actor Norton addressed these claims when explaining how elements of House of the Dragon come together.

offbeat.title: The Strokes: Reality Awaits review – a perfect marriage of mayhem and melody on their best album in years
offbeat.summary: H ere’s a sentence no old-head Strokes fan wants to hear: to understand Reality Awaits, the stalwart NYC group’s seventh album and first in six years, you first have to understand the Voidz. Julian Casablancas’s other band has been taking up more and more of his time in recent years, much to the chagrin of fans who may have dipped into the group’s painfully, awkwardly proggy 2014 debut Tyranny and decided to just let the old legend have his fun (away from their own speakers, of course).

offbeat.title: Antonelli reveals issue he must fix after father’s warning
offbeat.summary: Relationships between Formula 1 drivers and their fathers can be complicated. Sometimes the latter prefer to adopt a “tough love” approach that can raise a few eyebrows. Kimi Antonelli and his father Marco could be the poster boys for the opposite approach, such is the apparent closeness of their relationship from the outside. And yet, as the Mercedes driver revealed on media day in Hungary , his father is also his biggest critic – and isn’t afraid to say what he thinks. In this case the context was Antonelli’s predilection for making full use of track limits, something he has on occasion been penalised for.

offbeat.title: Revealed: Lorenzo's wild secret life before Love Island, with connections to Zayn Malik and Ekin-Su
offbeat.summary: It would seem that before Love Island 2026, Lorenzo Alessi had a wild life he’s been keeping quiet. When he was first introduced as part of the cast this year , Lorenzo was simply described as 28, and a business owner from Hertfordshire. However, he actually has multiple celeb connections, and a very vibey life that he’s swept under the rug. We all know that Lorenzo is a charmer, we’ve seen it in 4k every night, but it turns out he’s a very well connected man outside the villa. He has ties to lots of people, in high-up places. And they do say it’s all about who you know!

offbeat.title: Mikel Arteta axes three Arsenal stars ahead of new season as squad revamp gathers pace
offbeat.summary: Three Arsenal players have already been axed by Mikel Arteta as the Spaniard continues his major squad overhaul this summer . The Gunners boss went into this transfer window knowing he had to improve the quality of his squad in order to retain the Premier League title next season. The first step in achieving this is to offload players who are considered to fall short of the required standard, while the second is to bring in signings that address gaps in the squad. Jakub Kiwior was the first player shown the door by Arteta this summer. The Pole completed a permanent switch to Porto earlier this month for £14. 6million plus add-ons , following a loan spell with the Portuguese club last season.

offbeat.title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Review: Exactly What ‘Big Bang Theory’ Fans Deserve
offbeat.summary: If Sheldon, Leonard, Raj and Wolowitz were congregating around the couch eating takeout, “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” is exactly the kind of series they’d have on the TV. HBO Max’s 10-part “Big Bang Theory” spinoff revolves around comic book store owner Stuart David Bloom (Kevin Sussman) and launches in utter destruction, with zombies and giant moths that only bother you if you leave the lights on.
offbeat.trend: stuart fails to save the universe

offbeat.title: MetroStar's IRIS modernises US Air Force KC-135 tanker servicing
offbeat.summary: The US Air Force has purchased MetroStar and ActionStreamer’s new Integrated Respirator Information System (IRIS) to help its maintainers better service its fleet of KC-135 tankers . This is one of the first modernisations of a half-century-old maintenance process. USAF purchases Integrated Respirator Information System for KC-135 Last Monday, the US contractor MetroStar announced that Travis Air Force Base (home to many tanker aircraft) had purchased its Iris, an AI-powered maintenance product co-produced with ActionStreamer. These are purpose-built equipment for high-risk confined-space work.

offbeat.title: Why Cheddar’s partner Azad Cola moved to take over No.1 Oxford Street Hotel
offbeat.summary: Cola Holdings Limited secured a High Court order granting its Receiver police assistance to take control of the Osu hotel linked to Nana Kwame Bediako The dispute centres on an alleged debt Cola Holdings claims it settled on behalf of Kensington Residential Partners 1 Limited, the company behind the hotel Cheddar has disputed personal liability for the debt while KRP1 has filed an appeal and applied for interim relief pending the court's determination The No. 1 Oxford Street Hotel in Osu, Accra, has become the subject of a legal battle after Cola Holdings Limited moved to seize control of the luxury property associated with entrepreneur Nana Kwame Bediako , widely known as Cheddar.

offbeat.title: Malaysia moves closer to VL MICA missiles procurement for LCS
offbeat.summary: The Malaysian Ministry of Defence (MINDEF) has officially issued a Letter of Acceptance ( Surat Setuju Terima or SST) to MBDA France for the procurement of the VL MICA surface-to-air missile (SAM) system. The agreement was finalized during the opening day of the Farnborough International Airshow 2026 in the United Kingdom. A formal contract is still pending. The document was signed by MINDEF Secretary-General Datuk Lokman Hakim Ali on behalf of the Malaysian government.

offbeat.title: Julia Barretto Moves Effortlessly in Onitsuka Tiger Spring/Summer 2026 Collection
offbeat.summary: NOTE: BrandNews articles are promotional features from our sponsors and not news articles from our editorial staff. ABS-CBN Newsletter Your go-to source for the latest news, entertainment highlights, exclusive interviews, and updates on your favorite Kapamilya stars.

offbeat.title: What's that planned next to Pet Styles on Kirby Whitten in Bartlett?
offbeat.summary: Baptist Urgent Care plans to open a new walk-in clinic adjacent to Pet Styles Grooming Salon on Kirby Whitten road in Bartlett, opening mid-August and offering services for minor illnesses and injuries without appointments.

offbeat.title: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion?
offbeat.summary: Steve LePore | Coffee Bars or Compassion? An open letter to the pastors of Santa Clarita: “If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacking in daily food, and one of you says, ‘Go in peace, be warmed and filled,’ without giving them the things needed for the body, what good is that? ” — James 2:15-16. Here’s a question I can’t stop asking. What are your churches doing this week for the people in Santa Clarita who are too afraid to leave their homes? I’m not talking about politics. I’m not talking about immigration policy.

offbeat.title: Managing Liverpool as Rayan signing secured and four stars are ousted by Andoni Iraola
offbeat.summary: Andoni Iraola is gearing up for his first campaign as Liverpool manager. A blockbuster £100m-plus winger signing could be just what he needs to get his tenure rolling. On the back of a fairly disappointing season, Arne Slot is out and Iraola is in at Anfield. His squad have jetted off for their pre-season tour of the United States. When they return, regaining the Premier League crown will surely be the goal, with Champions League football to contend with as well. Yet, Mohamed Salah's, Andy Robertson's and Ibrahima Konate's exits have left voids that need filling. Jeremy Jacquet has been given the No.

offbeat.title: Unlike Joe Duffy, Kieran Cuddihy doesn’t ramp up the drama on Liveline
offbeat.summary: For any broadcaster, the ability to see both sides of the story is a crucial skill, but it’s mandatory on a show such as Liveline ( RTÉ Radio 1 , weekdays), where everyone basically thinks they’re in the right. Even so, Kieran Cuddihy goes above and beyond in his duty as host of the phone-in show, going one step farther by contradicting his own argument even as he makes it. On Tuesday, as callers debate whether helmets and high-vis gear should be compulsory for cyclists , Cuddihy suggests that legislating on such matters is meaningless without proper enforcement. As an example, he points to the legal requirement for lights on bikes after dark. “It’s not enforced.

offbeat.title: Stuttgart divided over Ermedin Demirovic’s future amid interest from Leeds and others
offbeat.summary: The Sky Germany Transfer Update Show reports that Ermedin Demirovic’s future at VfB Stuttgart has become the subject of internal debate amid interest from Juventus, Fenerbahçe and Leeds United. Evidently, head coach Sebastian Hoeneß is open to selling the 28-year-old. Stuttgart’s administrative team nevertheless retain a different view. Stuttgart are presently planning with a three-striker rotation featuring Demirovic, Deniz Undav and Wolfsburg talent Dzenan Pejcinovic. The latter has already agreed personal terms, though the clubs have yet to finalize a transfer. Jeremy Arevalo would serve as a developmental option, while Jovan Milosevic remains free to leave.

offbeat.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
offbeat.summary: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages. Iran says it has carried out drone strikes on US military facilities and bases across the Middle East in retaliation for the 13th consecutive night of American attacks on the country. Explosions were reported along Iran's Gulf coast, and in western, northern and central areas. Four people were killed and five injured in an attack in the south-west, Iranian state media said. Iran later announced strikes on US-allied Kuwait, while sirens sounded in Bahrain and Jordan, and flights were suspended at a major Iraqi airport after a reported drone crash.

offbeat.title: Why does Zapatero reappear now?
offbeat.summary: Why does Zapatero reappear now? This Thursday is an anniversary day. It has been exactly 26 years since José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero was elected secretary general of the PSOE. At that time, he did not imagine that a quarter of a century later he would be sitting in the studio of "Mañaneros" on TVE explaining himself for two serious criminal cases: one for alleged influence peddling for the Plus Ultra bailout and another for alleged smuggling of millionaire jewelry found in the safe of his office. Why has it appeared now? In fact, the explanations, according to what he himself had announced, should have come earlier.

offbeat.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
offbeat.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners in the latest escalation in the trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year. Those targeted, including the UK, China and the European Union, will face a tariff of 10% to 12. 5% on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. The tariffs, which replace an identical levy which expires on Friday, are based on claims key economic partners have failed to properly tackle forced labour.

offbeat.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
offbeat.summary: The divorce, involving the chairman of one of South Korea's biggest companies, has gripped the nation. The chairman of corporate giant SK Group has been ordered by a South Korean court to pay his ex-wife 944bn won (£483m; $644m) in a case that local media has called the " divorce of the century ". The award, which still needs to be finalised, is below the initial 1. 38tn won that chairman Chey Tae-won was told to pay Roh Soh-yeong in 2024. It comes more than a decade after his marriage to Roh - the daughter of a former president - fell apart following the revelation he had fathered a child with another woman.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street. Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee first launched in 2008 to respond to economic challenges around the UK. The meeting will bring together senior ministers and regional mayors to "put devolution at the centre of the government's mission to make Britain better". Ahead of that meeting, the prime minister said: "For 40 years, power and resources have been sucked into the centre, and too many communities have felt forgotten, without the attention and investment they deserve. " He added: "The days of Whitehall resisting devolution are over, for good.

politics.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
politics.summary: The housing secretary tells the BBC that the Renters Rights' Act is already having a significant impact on the market. Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has said Prime Minister Andy Burnham is not looking at bringing in rent controls in England. She told BBC Breakfast that changes made through the Renters' Rights Act, which came into effect in May, were "already having a significant impact on the market". She added that controls which had been implemented in some areas of the UK over the past few years had not "necessarily brought rents down". In 2023 when he was the mayor of Greater Manchester, Burnham called for freezes to private sector rents until the cost of living crisis was over.

politics.title: With Hopes High for New H.I.V. Prevention Pill, Merck Takes Steps to Ensure Access
politics.summary: Companies in Africa and India will make generic versions of the pill that could cost as little as $5 per person per year. But access to the drug and other new products in much of Latin America remains uncertain.

breaking.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
breaking.summary: Ramaphosa has faced scrutiny following the theft of large sums of cash from his private farm in 2020. A South African court has ruled in favour of President Cyril Ramaphosa's bid to temporarily halt a parliamentary inquiry looking into whether he should be impeached over what has been dubbed the "Farmgate" scandal. In 2022, an independent panel said Ramaphosa might have a case to answer after burglars stole huge sums of cash, hidden in a sofa, from his private farm. The president denied any wrongdoing. At the time MPs voted against establishing an impeachment inquiry, but in May the Constitutional Court ruled that parliament had acted unlawfully by shelving the panel's report.

breaking.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot as wildfires spread
breaking.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula. Further down the Atlantic coast, around the town of Biscarrosse in France's Landes region, another major fire that began on Thursday afternoon has prompted the evacuations of more than 23,000 people. About 18,000 people in Biscarrosse and 5,000 in Parentis-en-Born had to leave their homes, campsites, a nursing home and a summer camp. Landes prefect Gilles Clavreul said the fire around Biscarrosse was not going to be out by the end of Friday, with winds of up to 50km/h (31mph) and temperatures of 36-37C.

breaking.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters hold talks with government as stalemate continues
breaking.summary: The meeting came hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike. Thousands of people continue to protest demanding the Indian education minister's resignation after representatives of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) held talks with the government. CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka told reporters that the government has asked for a day to decide on their biggest demand - minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. "The protest will continue and will keep growing until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns. If that does not happen soon, we will be forced to announce another nationwide protest call," said Ranka.

crisis.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
crisis.summary: UK petrol and diesel prices are going up again as the price of oil returns to around $100 a barrel. According to the RAC, the average price of petrol reached an Iran war peak of 159. 53p a litre on 28 May, while diesel's highest average price during the conflict was 191. 54p a litre on 15 April. In early July, the RAC said the average price of diesel sank to a low of 150. 59p per litre for petrol and 164. 52p per litre for diesel. Since then the prices have been rising and according to its latest data, petrol now costs 156. 19p a litre while diesel costs 173. 15p a litre.

crisis.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
crisis.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

entertainment.title: Fred Tsui Named Executive Director of Hong Kong International Film Festival Society
entertainment.summary: The Hong Kong International Film Festival Society has appointed Fred Tsui , founder and CEO of Moebius Entertainment , as its new executive director, the organization said Friday. Tsui succeeds Albert Lee and assumes the role Sept. 23, reporting to HKIFFS chair Dr. Wilfred Wong and the board. In the new post, Tsui will oversee the society’s full range of initiatives: its marquee event, the Hong Kong International Film Festival, along with the Hong Kong – Asia Film Financing Forum and its HAF Film Lab, the Summer International Film Festival and the HKIFF Cine Fan strand.
entities: Hong Kong International Film Festival Society

sports.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
sports.summary: Jurgen Klopp starts his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. Jurgen Klopp started his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy. The former Liverpool manager was appointed on a four-year deal on Friday to replace Julian Nagelsmann, who resigned this month after Germany's shock World Cup defeat by Paraguay in the last 32. But the 59-year-old issued a warning to the media as he held his first press conference as Germany's head coach. "If you behave badly and don't leave my family in peace then I'll be gone, I'll just turn away," Klopp said.

sports.title: Meet the Glasgow curry shop boss representing Pakistan at bowls
sports.summary: Mohammed 'Chico' Qureshi has called Glasgow home for more than 50 years, but he will represent Pakistan against Scotland at the Commonwealth Games on Friday. It was something of a dream for Qureshi to go again in a second home Games after taking to the outdoor green at Kelvingrove 12 years ago. A member at Glasgow Indoor Bowling Club - just round the corner from his restaurant - he had been putting in the hours to be ready for the conditions awaiting him in his opening match against Wales' Ross Owen. Unfortunately, it did not quite go to plan as he lost in straight sets - 12-5 and 17-0. "That's the worst game I've ever played," a still upbeat Qureshi told BBC Scotland.

sports.title: Boxers choose Commonwealths over Love Island chance
sports.summary: Team Northern Ireland boxers Jon McConnell and Kaci Rock talk about choosing the Commonwealth Games over potentially appearing on Love Island. It could have been a very different summer for two members of Northern Ireland's 12-strong boxing team. Jon McConnell and Kaci Rock will compete in this year's Commonwealth Games for the first time, but had progressed through the initial auditions for the latest series of Love Island before pulling out because the TV show clashes in timing with the Games.

health.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
health.summary: ‘Bulking’, or gaining weight on purpose to get stronger, challenged everything I was once taught to do with my body I had been casually lifting weights for a few years when several things changed in quick succession. First, my boyfriend proposed. Continue reading. I had been casually lifting weights for a few years when several things changed in quick succession. This wasn’t a surprise – he had already told me he wanted to get married, and I had told him exactly how I wanted him to propose: somewhere public but not crowded, not in a restaurant.

health.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
health.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

health.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
health.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

tech.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
tech.summary: Microsoft has intervened to stop Windows 11 users with LG monitors from being bombarded with annoying McAfee trial pop-ups. In response to complaints about the LG bloatware, Microsoft’s Windows chief, Pavan Davuluri, said that LG has agreed to immediately disable the McAfee pop-up from its LG Monitor App Installer, and pledged that Microsoft will “keep improving here with our ecosystem partners. ” This crackdown follows backlash about large pop-up ads for McAfee antivirus and LG’s own apps appearing on Windows 11 PCs after being connected to LG monitors.

tech.title: An A.I. Music F.A.Q.: Can I Remix Madonna? Is This All Legal?
tech.summary: Advances in A.I. are making it possible to create all kinds of music from scratch, but they also raise questions about what is legal and who will actually listen.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

facts.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
facts.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

facts.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
facts.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.
